title: Refactor build orchestrator to reduce presubmit image footprint and introduce dependency fingerprinting
preview: Motivation: our presubmit images and toolchain bootstrap time have trended upward as we accumulate optional build helpers and devdeps. This PR refactors the CI build orchestrator to produce a smaller default presubmit image, introduce a dependency fingerprint index for targeted dep-refresh, and add a safe dep-prune ...
text:
Motivation: our presubmit images and toolchain bootstrap time have trended upward as we accumulate optional build helpers and devdeps. This PR refactors the CI build orchestrator to produce a smaller default presubmit image, introduce a dependency fingerprint index for targeted dep-refresh, and add a safe dep-prune pass that can be toggled per-team. The changes aim to reduce CI startup latency, make premerge effects of dependency updates auditable, and lower presubmit I/O on heavily changed branches.

Summary of changes: 1) Introduce 'slim-presubmit' microimage variant that omits optional language SDKs and heavy native toolchains; the orchestrator now selects slim vs full images based on profile tags in the monorepo manifest. 2) Add artifact-dep fingerprinting: CI computes a compact fingerprint of the graph (hash of lockfile + toolchain manifest + changed globs) and writes entries to ...

title: Adaptive compaction trigger and batch-aware spill controller
preview: Summary: This PR introduces two coordinated mechanisms to improve KV cache memory management under mixed workloads: 1) an adaptive compaction trigger that decides when to compact/merge small fragments based on a lightweight online heuristic, and 2) a batch-aware spill controller that paces spill operations according...
text:
Summary: This PR introduces two coordinated mechanisms to improve KV cache memory management under mixed workloads: 1) an adaptive compaction trigger that decides when to compact/merge small fragments based on a lightweight online heuristic, and 2) a batch-aware spill controller that paces spill operations according to continuous-batching pressure and kernel utilization.

Motivation: We observed repeated p99 latency spikes during sustained continuous-batching workloads caused by synchronous spill/compaction activity that collided with kernel hot paths. Compaction is beneficial for long-term fragmentation reduction but can cause synchronous work and page movement at inopportune times. The new approach learns a compact/skip decision from running statistics (fragmentation score, eviction pressure, recent page churn) and cooperates with the request scheduler so compaction work is deferred...

title: Isolated-region protection and safe-fallback with KV-cache drain (hotfix)
preview: Motivation: During a cross-region incident we observed an "islanded" region scenario where the control plane-to-node heartbeats transiently lost egress but local model processes remained healthy. That caused the routing layer to continue directing requests to a region that could not reach shared KV/cache invalidatio...
text: Motivation: During a cross-region incident we observed an "islanded" region scenario where the control plane-to-node heartbeats transiently lost egress but local model processes remained healthy. That caused the routing layer to continue directing requests to a region that could not reach shared KV/cache invalidation, resulting in stale prefix reuse and increased error rates for model variants dependent on external context. This PR implements a detection-and-protect hotfix: (1) islanded region detector with conservative hysteresis, (2) a 'safe-fallback' routing policy that redirects new requests to healthy regions while letting in-flight streams finish, and (3) a KV-cache drain path for the local runtime to evict prefix/KV entries that could become stale when the region is isolated. The change is intentionally additive and guarded by an operator-configurable feature flag. Summary of c...

title: introduce-stage-aware-ci-fanout-and-dependency-compactifier
preview: Motivation: feedback from large teams and recurring release friction showed two pain points: (1) presubmit lanes run too many linear checks for small changes, increasing latency and contributor churn; (2) devdeps and monorepo surface area have accumulated transitive baggage making CI images and cache warm slower. Th...
text:
Motivation: feedback from large teams and recurring release friction showed two pain points: (1) presubmit lanes run too many linear checks for small changes, increasing latency and contributor churn; (2) devdeps and monorepo surface area have accumulated transitive baggage making CI images and cache warm slower. This PR introduces a stage-aware CI fanout orchestrator and a dependency compactifier that trims dev-only transitive deps from CI images and lockfile footprints. Goals: reduce median premerge turnaround by ~30%, shrink CI image build time, and make dependency updates less noisy. Summary of changes:
- Add ci/fanout-controller: lightweight orchestrator that maps PRs to targeted premerge lanes based on path heuristics and changed module manifests. Controls fanout degree per-stage (lint/unit/integration) and supports fast-fail propagation.
- Introduce tooling/dep-compactifier: Go...

title: Introduce batch-affinity optimizer and 'quant-sentinel' graceful quantization profile
preview: Motivation: We have recurring tenant noise and suboptimal packing when multiple small requests with similar token shapes hit the same serving pool. Current batching is agnostic to tenant affinity which causes more KV cache misses and inefficient GPU utilization. Additionally, aggressive quantization rollouts have ca...
text: Motivation: We have recurring tenant noise and suboptimal packing when multiple small requests with similar token shapes hit the same serving pool. Current batching is agnostic to tenant affinity which causes more KV cache misses and inefficient GPU utilization. Additionally, aggressive quantization rollouts have caused occasional quality regressions for tail workloads. This PR introduces two coordinated features: (1) a batch-affinity optimizer that prefers packing requests with high locality (tenant, route, prompt-shape signatures) while respecting latency SLOs and (2) a conservative 'quant-sentinel' quantization profile that progressively reduces precision by lane and includes a rollback sentinel that forces fallback to a safe quantized variant on quality regressions. Design/changes summary: - Routing/packing: new affinity scoring module (affinity_score) that computes a lightweight ...

title: compat: strict media negotiation + per-file ingestion-rate-limits for tool attachments
preview: Context: Our OpenAI-compat layer has had recurring issues when clients upload ambiguous or binary attachments without clear content-type or length headers. These cause parsing retries, confused streaming boundaries, and occasional function_call failures when tool runtimes receive corrupted payloads. This PR introduc...
text:
Context: Our OpenAI-compat layer has had recurring issues when clients upload ambiguous or binary attachments without clear content-type or length headers. These cause parsing retries, confused streaming boundaries, and occasional function_call failures when tool runtimes receive corrupted payloads. This PR introduces two complementary protections: (1) deterministic media negotiation and normalization so the compat layer always annotates attachments with a canonical content-type + checksum; (2) per-file ingestion rate limits and adaptive chunk checks to prevent slow/bursty clients from overwhelming streaming parsers and downstream tool runtimes.

What this changes (high level):
- Compat layer now enforces an Accept/Content-Type negotiation step for incoming attachments. Preferred content-types for tool calls are application/json (pointer/meta), application/octet-stream (binary payload...

title: v1/chat: stabilize streaming handshake; enforce stop-token precedence and introduce role-alias mapping
preview: Context: Customers integrating via the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions surface have seen two classes of issues in production: 1) transient stream disconnects during tool/function call handoffs (client resumes see inconsistent framing), and 2) ambiguity in how stop tokens are applied when stop arrays are provided alongsi...
text: Context: Customers integrating via the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions surface have seen two classes of issues in production: 1) transient stream disconnects during tool/function call handoffs (client resumes see inconsistent framing), and 2) ambiguity in how stop tokens are applied when stop arrays are provided alongside role-level stop hints or tool-generated token sequences. This PR unifies several compatibility behaviors to reduce surprise for downstream clients and to harden streaming handshakes during function/tool call routing. Motivation: Provide predictable, backward-compatible semantics for v1 chat parity while reducing the failure surface during streaming toolcalls. Goals: (a) stabilize the streaming handshake so that tool/function call initiation and the subsequent resumption of the assistant stream never produce overlapping or dropped deltas, (b) define and implement a determ...

title: quarantine-sse-chunks-and-delimiter-healing-for-partial-json
preview: Context and motivation: We observed intermittent client-side crashes and invalid JSON errors when streaming long chat responses through the OpenAI-compat SSE adapter. Root cause: a mix of small TCP packet boundaries, server-side aggressive chunking, and occasional delimiter bytes embedded in model token escapes resu...
text:
Context and motivation: We observed intermittent client-side crashes and invalid JSON errors when streaming long chat responses through the OpenAI-compat SSE adapter. Root cause: a mix of small TCP packet boundaries, server-side aggressive chunking, and occasional delimiter bytes embedded in model token escapes resulted in mid-JSON fragments being emitted without clear boundaries. Prior fixes focused on delimiting heuristics but still allowed tight races where a fragment split removed the terminal '}' or a tool-call sentinel.

This PR implements a two-part strategy: (1) a quarantining window for suspect fragments so the parser doesn't emit partial JSON to higher layers until it has greater confidence; (2) a delimiter-healing state machine that uses lightweight delimiter fingerprints, sequence-checkpoint hints, and backoff/hedging to reconstruct or request missing spans where possible....

title: credential-sanity-linter-and-sdk-interop-polish
preview: Motivation: Teams keep hitting subtle auth edge-cases when swapping credential providers across SDKs (Python/TypeScript/Go). This patch introduces a credential-sanity linter, cross-SDK interop smoke tests, and a small set of streaming API ergonomics fixes and version bumps. The goal is to reduce incidents caused by ...
text:
Motivation: Teams keep hitting subtle auth edge-cases when swapping credential providers across SDKs (Python/TypeScript/Go). This patch introduces a credential-sanity linter, cross-SDK interop smoke tests, and a small set of streaming API ergonomics fixes and version bumps. The goal is to reduce incidents caused by mis-typed credential payloads and to make streaming helpers more uniform across languages. Summary of changes below with checklists and upgrade guidance.

What this PR does: Adds a new repo-level tool (tools/cred-lint) that validates credential payloads and common misconfigurations used in our SDK tests; standardizes streaming helper names and optional parameters across sdks/; adds interop smoke tests that call the same scripted scenarios from each SDK variant; bumps SDK packaged versions to pick up ergonomics helpers; fixes a handful of flaky reauth races visible in CI.

C...

title: Longtail SLO meter and annotation adapter
preview: Motivation: We have seen sporadic, low-volume prompt-set failures that escape per-run thresholds but aggregate into customer-visible issues over time (long tail). This PR implements a Longtail SLO Meter that maintains a decay-weighted time series of failure signals and a companion Annotation Adapter to attach contex...
text: Motivation: We have seen sporadic, low-volume prompt-set failures that escape per-run thresholds but aggregate into customer-visible issues over time (long tail). This PR implements a Longtail SLO Meter that maintains a decay-weighted time series of failure signals and a companion Annotation Adapter to attach contextual evidence (examples, model variant, env) to flagged prompt-sets. The goal is to surface gradual SLO drift earlier and enable actionable handoff to triage workflows without increasing noisy CI failures. Design summary: 1) longtail_meter module: computes an exponentially decayed aggregate for each prompt-cohort keyed by prompt_set_id + model_variant, supports configurable half-life and bucket lengths; 2) annotation_adapter: lightweight pluggable interface (sync+async) that writes structured evidence to eval metadata and an optional annotations store; 3) ci-integration: a ...

title: Lockstep SSE windowing and function-call handoff to prevent partial JSON emission
preview: Context: Intermittent user reports and internal load tests showed that long-lived SSE connections occasionally emitted fragments that, when consumed by OpenAI-compatible clients, produced partial JSON objects for function/tool call payloads. Root cause: the previous coalescing strategy favored throughput by merging ...
text:
Context: Intermittent user reports and internal load tests showed that long-lived SSE connections occasionally emitted fragments that, when consumed by OpenAI-compatible clients, produced partial JSON objects for function/tool call payloads. Root cause: the previous coalescing strategy favored throughput by merging fragments eagerly, and the streaming parser could flush partial JSON tokens when a function-call sentinel spanned a fragmentation boundary. This caused downstream consumers that assume atomic JSON frames to throw parse errors and led to retries and duplicated tool calls.

Motivation: We need a conservative, low-risk change to the SSE emission pipeline that prevents partial JSON emission for tool/function call boundaries while preserving overall throughput and backpressure characteristics. This PR implements a lockstep windowing approach: event fragments that may contain fun...

title: align openai /v1/chat.completions role semantics, stop/logprobs and structured-response framing
preview: Motivation: Customers expect exact parity with OpenAI's /v1/chat.completions for role handling, stop sequences, and logprobs output shape. We also have recurring streaming edgecases where role tokens arrive as separate SSE events leading to downstream SDKs mis-attributing first assistant delta. This PR harmonizes th...
text: Motivation: Customers expect exact parity with OpenAI's /v1/chat.completions for role handling, stop sequences, and logprobs output shape. We also have recurring streaming edgecases where role tokens arrive as separate SSE events leading to downstream SDKs mis-attributing first assistant delta. This PR harmonizes the OpenAI-compat layer to produce: 1) canonical role deltas where the initial assistant role is coalesced into the first token event; 2) explicit mapping for 'stop' as either string or array and consistent enforcement across server- and client-side truncation; 3) support for returning token-level logprobs and top_k entries when logprobs>0 is requested; 4) a structured-output framing option that injects a stable JSON wrapper event and schema hint to help SDKs auto-parse structured responses. Changes are backward-compatible by default: legacy clients keep previous behavior unl...

title: manifest-diff-builder and KMS-stamped attestations for incremental private-installer releases
preview: Motivation: make private-installer releases incremental and cryptographically auditable without re-bundling full artifacts for every patch. This PR implements a manifest-diff builder along with KMS-stamped attestations for every delta to enable fast rollforward/rollback and compact provenance storage. Summary: add a...
text: Motivation: make private-installer releases incremental and cryptographically auditable without re-bundling full artifacts for every patch. This PR implements a manifest-diff builder along with KMS-stamped attestations for every delta to enable fast rollforward/rollback and compact provenance storage. Summary: add a new release tool 'manifest-diff' that computes a deterministic delta index between two installer builds, produce a compact delta tarball and an accompanying attest.json signed via customer-configured KMS (supports AWS KMS and Vault HSM). The pipeline integrates the diff artifacts into the release graph and updates release-manifests.yaml with delta entries and signature metadata. Commits summary: 1) initial manifest-diff implementation and tests; 2) KMS attestation client and integration layer; 3) CI pipeline changes to upload delta artifacts to release-bucket and verify at...

title: Resumable streaming: disconnect quarantine, server timeouts, and function-call idempotency
preview: Context and motivation: Production customers hit a class of errors where brief client disconnects (mobile network flaps, browser tab swaps) caused long-running streaming requests that invoked tools to either get duplicated or be lost after client reconnect. Existing resume tokens preserved raw streaming offsets but ...
text:
Context and motivation: Production customers hit a class of errors where brief client disconnects (mobile network flaps, browser tab swaps) caused long-running streaming requests that invoked tools to either get duplicated or be lost after client reconnect. Existing resume tokens preserved raw streaming offsets but did not maintain function-call intent or partial outputs, making resuming tool invocations unsafe. This PR introduces a small set of changes to make streaming resumes predictable and safe for function/tool calls and structured output in the OpenAI compatibility layer.

What this change does (single continuous description): Adds a server-side "reconnect quarantine" (default 20s) during which a session can reconnect and claim in-flight stream state; introduces a server-enforced soft-cancel TTL for tool/function calls that allows the runtime to decide whether to allow the tool...

title: Introduce Toolcall Conductor with Async Signal Latching and OpenAI-compatible Result Hydration
preview: Context: Customers increasingly run multi-tool workflows where the model issues parallel function/tool calls and expects deterministic result composition, streaming stability, and graceful retries. Motivation: previous ad-hoc orchestration led to gibberish structured outputs, duplicate side-effects when retries happ...
text: Context: Customers increasingly run multi-tool workflows where the model issues parallel function/tool calls and expects deterministic result composition, streaming stability, and graceful retries. Motivation: previous ad-hoc orchestration led to gibberish structured outputs, duplicate side-effects when retries happened, and brittle OpenAI-compat behavior for function-calling sequences. This PR introduces a new lightweight Toolcall Conductor that centralizes execution ergonomics: async signal latching to coordinate parallel tool runs, idempotent result hydration to prevent duplicate injections, and an OpenAI-compat shim for function-calling semantics in streamed responses. Summary of changes: 1) runtime: add redwood/pkg/runtime/toolcall/conductor.go implementing a conductor that tracks call IDs, latches result signals, and emits ordered hydration events to the streaming layer. 2) api:...

title: prefill-granularity-adaptation-and-decode-slip-controller
preview: Context: Large variance in sequence-length mixes causes the static-chunking heuristic to either over-prefill (high tail latency) or under-utilize kernels (reduced throughput). This PR introduces two coordinated mechanisms: 1) a prefill granularity adapter that tunes chunk sizes per-worker based on online sequence-le...
text: Context: Large variance in sequence-length mixes causes the static-chunking heuristic to either over-prefill (high tail latency) or under-utilize kernels (reduced throughput). This PR introduces two coordinated mechanisms: 1) a prefill granularity adapter that tunes chunk sizes per-worker based on online sequence-length statistics with hysteresis to avoid oscillation; 2) a decode-slip controller that detects and corrects 'decode slips' where decode work lags prefill progress, by selectively throttling new prefill chunks and prioritizing KV preheat for imminent decodes. Motivation: reduce p95/p99 tail latencies without sacrificing average throughput across mixed workloads. Summary of changes: - runtime/scheduler: add prefill_resizer module that maintains an EWMA of token lengths and chooses chunk targets; includes hysteresis thresholds and a bounded resize rate to keep stability; imple...

title: Sticky circuit rescue and tenant SLO lease reconciliation hotpatch
preview: Context: During a regional failover test we observed a class of "sticky" circuit states where a tenant's circuit stayed open on a subset of nodes because SLO lease renewal metadata was lost during transient control-plane reconvergence. This caused two poor outcomes: (1) recovery attempts kept evicting traffic from t...
text: Context: During a regional failover test we observed a class of "sticky" circuit states where a tenant's circuit stayed open on a subset of nodes because SLO lease renewal metadata was lost during transient control-plane reconvergence. This caused two poor outcomes: (1) recovery attempts kept evicting traffic from the recovering cohort and (2) telemetry showed mismatched SLO ownership leading to incorrect load-shedding decisions. Motivation: quick mitigation for production incidents plus a robust reconciliation path to prevent recurrence. This PR implements a two-part hotpatch: (A) Rescue path in the circuit manager that forces a short idempotent reconciliation handshake between the regional controller and node-local circuit store when a lease renewal is missing for >30s, and (B) SLO lease reconciliation routine that scans owner metadata and repairs mismatched leases with a conservati...

title: Policy-driven gradient rollouts and shadow/A-B traffic orchestration
preview: Motivation: provide a single policy layer that can express canary gradients, A/B splits, shadowing, and automated rollback triggers for model variant rollouts across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments. This PR implements a policy-driven rollout engine and runtime hooks to route traffic incrementally, evaluat...
text:
Motivation: provide a single policy layer that can express canary gradients, A/B splits, shadowing, and automated rollback triggers for model variant rollouts across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments. This PR implements a policy-driven rollout engine and runtime hooks to route traffic incrementally, evaluate health metrics, and trigger automated rollback or promotion when thresholds are met.

What this change contains: checklist: [x] rollout policy DSL and parser; [x] runtime router integration (routing-by-policy); [x] metrics adapter to evaluate success criteria (latency, error-rate, quality-regression hooks); [x] shadow mode implementation (non-impacting mirrored requests); [x] A/B split support with weighted sampling; [x] automated rollback circuit with hysteresis and cooldown; [x] console integration for visualizing rollout state and editing policies; [x] e2e and unit tes...

title: introduce-session-lease-shim-cross-sdk-ergonomics-and-version-bumps
preview: Motivation: We continue to see transient spikes in reauth calls and duplicated token refresh logic across SDKs, which increases tail latency for streaming workloads and complicates operator mental models. This PR introduces a cross-SDK 'session lease' shim: a short-lived leased token that the SDK refreshes proactive...
text:
Motivation: We continue to see transient spikes in reauth calls and duplicated token refresh logic across SDKs, which increases tail latency for streaming workloads and complicates operator mental models. This PR introduces a cross-SDK 'session lease' shim: a short-lived leased token that the SDK refreshes proactively on a coordinated cadence, together with a small state machine that exposes lease health to the streaming layer. The goal is to reduce auth thrash, align retry/backoff semantics across languages, and provide ergonomic stream handles for application authors.

What this PR does (single-string summary): Adds SessionLeaseManager to Python, a Lease-aware Transport and StreamHandle to TypeScript, and a lease-enabled connection pool in Go. Harmonizes retry windows and backoff formulas for auth failures vs transient network errors. Adds ergonomic stream APIs: Python async iterato...

title: Introduce lightweight lint runtime pool and dependency grooming task
preview: Problem: presubmit lint jobs are expensive and noisy; transient dependency spikes increase CI latency. This PR introduces a lightweight, long-lived lint runtime pool (shared JVM/Node environments) plus a scheduled dependency grooming task to keep devdeps compact. Goals: reduce presubmit latency variance, cut install...
text:
Problem: presubmit lint jobs are expensive and noisy; transient dependency spikes increase CI latency. This PR introduces a lightweight, long-lived lint runtime pool (shared JVM/Node environments) plus a scheduled dependency grooming task to keep devdeps compact. Goals: reduce presubmit latency variance, cut install IO, and make small dependency churn visible for engineering teams.

What this PR does (summary):
- Adds a new presubmit runner material: .github/workflows/presubmit-lightweight.yml that routes lint-only PRs into the lightweight pool.
- Implements a lint pool coordinator: ci/lint/pool_coordinator.go (small state machine, in-memory leases with optional Redis backing).
- Replaces heavy per-job toolchain bootstrap for JS/TS linters with a cached shared environment (ci/images/presubmit-lint.tar).
- Adds tools/dep-groom.sh, a scheduled task (GH Action scheduled workflow) that ru...

title: Introduce slim presubmit quickcheck and dependency entropy mitigator
preview: Context: We are observing long presubmit runtime spikes and noisy dependency churn which cause slower feedback and occasional release regressions. This PR introduces two coordinated interventions: 1) a 'quickcheck' presubmit lane that runs a tiny, fast smoke subset of lint+unit checks for rapid developer feedback, d...
text:
Context: We are observing long presubmit runtime spikes and noisy dependency churn which cause slower feedback and occasional release regressions. This PR introduces two coordinated interventions: 1) a 'quickcheck' presubmit lane that runs a tiny, fast smoke subset of lint+unit checks for rapid developer feedback, dynamically escalating to full presubmit on larger diffs; 2) a Dependency Entropy Mitigator (DE-Mitigator), a policy + CI job that detects high-velocity transitive changes and applies conservative auto-fix suggestions (lockfile pinning, devdep pruning), plus a dry-run bot that submits candidate fixes for approval.

What I changed (high level): Consolidated presubmit orchestration into an explicit stage graph (presubmit.quickcheck -> presubmit.full -> presubmit.post-merge), added a tiny Node/Go lint shard runner and a lightweight python test-sandbox for fast isolation, implem...

title: Multiplexer: parallel tool execution, idempotent retries, and ergonomic result injection
preview: Motivation: production workloads increasingly issue multiple tool calls per response (lookups, enrichment, external actions) and clients expect predictable injection semantics, low-latency parallelism, and transparent retry behavior when external tools fail. This PR introduces a lightweight multiplexer for tool exec...
text:
Motivation: production workloads increasingly issue multiple tool calls per response (lookups, enrichment, external actions) and clients expect predictable injection semantics, low-latency parallelism, and transparent retry behavior when external tools fail. This PR introduces a lightweight multiplexer for tool execution that: 1) executes independent tool calls in parallel when safe, 2) exposes an explicit idempotency token + retry middleware for transient tool failures, 3) provides an ergonomic result-injection API surface for handlers (deterministic injection points with ordering hints), and 4) keeps OpenAI-compatibility behaviors (function-calling fallback and structured outputs) intact.

Summary of changes: commits implement a runtime-level ToolMultiplexer that accepts a list of ToolCall requests and returns deterministic ToolResults with stable ids and insertion points. We added ...

title: soft-slot-compaction-and-reverse-probe-continuous-batcher-tuning
preview: Motivation: tail latency and occasional starvation remain a concern for long-lived continuous batching workloads that mix very small and very large KV-aware requests. Existing admission logic can leave low-priority small requests waiting behind large KV-heavy slots for extended periods, especially under transient lo...
text: Motivation: tail latency and occasional starvation remain a concern for long-lived continuous batching workloads that mix very small and very large KV-aware requests. Existing admission logic can leave low-priority small requests waiting behind large KV-heavy slots for extended periods, especially under transient load spikes and kernel-switch latencies. This PR introduces two coordinated mechanisms: 1) Soft slot compaction: a background compaction pass that opportunistically collapses underutilized slots to reduce fragmentation and improve batch packing without violating latency SLOs; 2) Reverse probe (starvation recovery probe): a low-overhead probe that periodically injects high-priority short-circuit admissions from the tail of the queue to validate progress and trigger rebalancing when starvation is detected. Design summary: soft slot compaction tracks slot utilization windows and...

title: v1/chat.completions: resilient stream-fragmenting + truncated-logprobs + toolcall backoff
preview: Motivation: We've observed two classes of production failures in the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions compatibility adapter when tool/function calling and long-running model responses are involved: 1) clients receiving partial or duplicated streamed chunks after transient network blips or internal retries, and 2) malform...
text:
Motivation: We've observed two classes of production failures in the OpenAI /v1/chat.completions compatibility adapter when tool/function calling and long-running model responses are involved: 1) clients receiving partial or duplicated streamed chunks after transient network blips or internal retries, and 2) malformed structured outputs (JSON) emitted incrementally by the model which break downstream tool parsing. Additionally, customers running toolcall heavy workloads saw elevated tail error rates when the function invocation subsystem experienced transient 5xxs or long latencies.

This PR implements a set of protocol and runtime changes to improve UX and parity with typical OpenAI semantics while keeping streaming stable:
- Stream fragment reconciliation: attach a "stream_fragment_id" UUID to every top-level event in the stream envelope and an optional "resume_token" when resuming....

title: dynamic per-channel requant + KV-aware jitter-resilient continuous-batching
preview: Background and motivation: Production workloads show variance in per-channel activation distributions across prompts and token positions which makes static requant scales suboptimal. We observed systematic p99 latency spikes when the runtime switched between hot and cold KV cache footprints under continuous-batching...
text: Background and motivation: Production workloads show variance in per-channel activation distributions across prompts and token positions which makes static requant scales suboptimal. We observed systematic p99 latency spikes when the runtime switched between hot and cold KV cache footprints under continuous-batching; these were exacerbated by small batching jitter when multiple streams aligned. This PR implements a dynamic per-channel requant stage combined with a KV-cache-aware scheduling hint and a jitter-resilient continuous-batching policy to smooth throughput while keeping low tail latency. Scope: 1) A lightweight per-channel scale estimator that runs on the microkernel hot path and updates requant multipliers at configurable amortization windows. 2) A cheap, lock-free KV cache sizing hint that the scheduler uses to avoid abrupt prefix evictions during batch coalescing. 3) A jitt...

title: hybrid flashattn + lazy-kv-eviction + operator-fusion for long-seq workloads
preview: Motivation: Production workloads with mixed short- and long-context requests show uneven tail latency and high aggregate token cost due to conservative KV cache retention and generic attention kernel selection. This PR introduces a hybrid attention path that (1) dispatches FlashAttention when safe, (2) uses a lightw...
text:
Motivation: Production workloads with mixed short- and long-context requests show uneven tail latency and high aggregate token cost due to conservative KV cache retention and generic attention kernel selection. This PR introduces a hybrid attention path that (1) dispatches FlashAttention when safe, (2) uses a lightweight fused matmul-add-dropout kernel for mid-sized heads, and (3) falls back to the existing fallback kernel for pathological shapes. In parallel, we implement a lazy KV eviction policy that decouples KV entry lifetime from request completion and enables opportunistic eviction under memory pressure. Together these changes reduce long-sequence p99 latency and improve throughput for streaming workloads while keeping memory bounded.

What changed (high level): implemented a Flash-aware scheduler that picks kernel variant at invocation time based on sequence length, head size,...

title: Adaptive jittered flush and downstream-aware credit pacing for smoother streaming
preview: Summary: Implements three coordinated runtime changes to reduce streaming tail and burst latency for token-generation workloads: (1) a jittered flush window that spreads microbatch flushes to avoid aligned burst tails; (2) a downstream-latency-aware credit allocator that paces token credits based on observed consume...
text: Summary: Implements three coordinated runtime changes to reduce streaming tail and burst latency for token-generation workloads: (1) a jittered flush window that spreads microbatch flushes to avoid aligned burst tails; (2) a downstream-latency-aware credit allocator that paces token credits based on observed consumer RTT and in-flight decode headroom; (3) a speculative KV prefix hint prefetch that warms per-GPU KV cache for likely next-token prefixes using a lightweight in-memory prefix index. Goal: smooth token emission, reduce 95/99p TTFB for streaming clients, and avoid unnecessary kernel wakeups while keeping median latency neutral. Motivation and context: Production canaries and incidents (ENG-4823, ENG-4899) showed recurring tail spikes when many concurrent streams synchronized flushes at token boundaries or periodic intervals. Prior PRs mitigated parts of this, but correlation ...

title: Improve chat stream framing, function-call handshake, and logprob envelope
preview: Motivation: We observed intermittent corruption of streamed messages when function calls were emitted mid-chunk and when stop sequences split across buffer boundaries. Consumers (third-party SDKs and internal examples) reported mis-attributed logprobs and truncated function-call payloads. This causes downstream tool...
text:
Motivation: We observed intermittent corruption of streamed messages when function calls were emitted mid-chunk and when stop sequences split across buffer boundaries. Consumers (third-party SDKs and internal examples) reported mis-attributed logprobs and truncated function-call payloads. This causes downstream tooling that relies on deterministic final outputs and token-level diagnostics to misbehave.

Changes in this PR: added a lightweight framing state machine to the streaming runtime so every SSE/data chunk is annotated with a stable boundary token and an explicit event for function-call lifecycle: 'fn_call.start' and 'fn_call.end'. This removes ambiguity about when a tool invocation begins and ends across stream re-fragments. To surface token-level diagnostics without polluting the incremental stream we emit a final 'logprob_envelope' JSON object as the last event for both strea...

title: concurrent-tool-runner: parallel tool invocation + OpenAI function_call compatibility updates
preview: Motivation: customers increasingly chain multiple tool calls per turn and want lower tail latency by running compatible tools in parallel, plus predictable result injection and idempotent retries. This PR introduces a server-side concurrent tool runner, extends the OpenAI-compatible function_call surface to allow ex...
text: Motivation: customers increasingly chain multiple tool calls per turn and want lower tail latency by running compatible tools in parallel, plus predictable result injection and idempotent retries. This PR introduces a server-side concurrent tool runner, extends the OpenAI-compatible function_call surface to allow explicit parallel calls and tool-result references, and updates SDK helpers and streaming merge semantics to keep the API stable for existing clients. Summary of changes: 1) runtime: add services/inference/tool_runner/concurrent_runner with a pluggable execution strategy (sequential | parallel | hybrid), a run_id-based idempotency layer for retries, and deterministic merge ordering for structured output injection. 2) api/openai-compat: extend function_call payload with optional keys: "parallel_calls" (array of tool names), "tool_result_refs" (map-like list of result identifie...

title: introduce-warmth-oracle-and-burst-fidelity-adapter
preview: Context and motivation: large tenants and bursty workloads create a repeated operational pattern: 1) cold clusters take time to reach steady KV-cache and batching efficiency, 2) ad-hoc bursts either exhaust soft quotas or produce high-per-tenant latency spikes, and 3) telemetry shows large oscillations in utilizatio...
text: Context and motivation: large tenants and bursty workloads create a repeated operational pattern: 1) cold clusters take time to reach steady KV-cache and batching efficiency, 2) ad-hoc bursts either exhaust soft quotas or produce high-per-tenant latency spikes, and 3) telemetry shows large oscillations in utilization that make predictive autoscaling noisy. This PR introduces two cooperating components — the Warmth Oracle and the Burst Fidelity Adapter — to reduce cold-start cost, stabilize autoscaling signals, and enforce tenant-level burst fidelity guarantees without hard-cap preemption. Goals: reduce cold-start tail latency for bursty tenants, smooth utilization signals used by the autoscaler, and provide per-tenant fidelity telemetry so SREs/product teams can reason about burst degradation. What this implements: - Warmth Oracle: lightweight estimator that maintains a time-decayed w...

title: reservation-triggered-throttle-and-utilization-wavelet-reporter
preview: Context: Dedicated fleets sometimes oscillate between being underutilized and briefly saturated during short bursts. Existing burst controls rely on global accounting windows which either respond too late or over-aggressively for reservation-backed traffic. This PR introduces a reservation-triggered throttle that ac...
text: Context: Dedicated fleets sometimes oscillate between being underutilized and briefly saturated during short bursts. Existing burst controls rely on global accounting windows which either respond too late or over-aggressively for reservation-backed traffic. This PR introduces a reservation-triggered throttle that activates when a reservation's consumption pattern crosses configurable safety thresholds, and a complementary utilization wavelet reporter that provides smoothed, multi-scale utilization signals for autoscalers and SLO monitors. Motivation: reduce noisy evictions and over-provisioning, improve SLO preservation for reservation-backed tenants, and provide more actionable utilization telemetry for downstream controllers. What this changes: 1) Runtime: add a reservation-level throttle controller that monitors per-reservation token/slot consumption and emits soft-throttle decisio...

title: stochastic-fallback-admitter-for-continuous-batcher-starvation-mitigation
preview: Context: Production traffic patterns exposed intermittent starvation of KV-heavy requests in continuous-batch workers when a small number of long-lived KV-pin flows monopolized slots. This caused tail latency spikes under moderate load even though median throughput was fine. Motivation: Introduce a lightweight runti...
text: Context: Production traffic patterns exposed intermittent starvation of KV-heavy requests in continuous-batch workers when a small number of long-lived KV-pin flows monopolized slots. This caused tail latency spikes under moderate load even though median throughput was fine. Motivation: Introduce a lightweight runtime admission policy that probabilistically relaxes strict slot reservations and induces controlled fallbacks to non-prefetch paths for dominated flows, reducing starvation without regressing steady-state throughput. Design summary: add a StochasticFallbackAdmitter that observes per-slot hold durations and per-flow KV-saturation score, computes a time-decayed weight, and performs a small-probability fallback decision when a scorer exceeds a runtime-configurable threshold. The admitter is integrated into the continuous-batcher admission path with two knobs: fallback_probabili...

title: introduce-server-timebox-and-idempotent-cancel-fallbacks-for-streaming-compat
preview: Context: production customers observed cases where long lived streaming sessions (tool calls + structured output) were left in indeterminate states after a client network disconnect or a client-initiated Cancel. Motivation: we need a predictable server-side safety net that bounds resource residency time, ensures too...
text: Context: production customers observed cases where long lived streaming sessions (tool calls + structured output) were left in indeterminate states after a client network disconnect or a client-initiated Cancel. Motivation: we need a predictable server-side safety net that bounds resource residency time, ensures tool-call idempotency when retries/resume occur, and provides a clear fallback path (trimmed response + final event) that OpenAI-compatible clients can rely on. This PR implements a server-side streaming timebox (configurable per-route) that gracefully converts oversized/inflight streams into a final 'timeboxed' terminal event, augments client-cancel handling to be idempotent across reconnect/resume windows, and plumbs a compact resume token that includes tool-call idempotency metadata. Summary of changes: 1) stream manager: add configurable timebox_ms and a deterministic fina...

title: Introduce stream-proxy: preflight cred-priming, heartbeat publishers, and tri-SDK version bumps
preview: Problem: multiple SDKs independently were doing cred pre-warm, heartbeat, and streamed telemetry which caused duplicated requests, token churn, and mismatched heartbeat intervals across languages. This led to spiky auth calls under bursty workloads and noisy telemetry. Goal: provide a single lightweight in-repo "str...
text: Problem: multiple SDKs independently were doing cred pre-warm, heartbeat, and streamed telemetry which caused duplicated requests, token churn, and mismatched heartbeat intervals across languages. This led to spiky auth calls under bursty workloads and noisy telemetry. Goal: provide a single lightweight in-repo "stream-proxy" primitive that centralizes preflight credential priming, lightweight heartbeat publishers for long-lived streams, and a compact telemetry envelope for streamed metrics. The change harmonizes behavior across Python/TypeScript/Go SDKs, reduces auth thundering, and enables a safer default for session lifetime handling. Summary of changes: - Add a small in-repo stream-proxy component (lib/stream_proxy/) that exposes an opinionated REST-compatible boundary used by SDK adapters. The proxy supports: cred-priming (pre-warm token and short-lived lease), heartbeat publishe...

title: Introduce contextual cohort synthesis gate with adaptive weighting
preview: Motivation: We frequently see spurious regression signals when prompt distributions shift subtly (new metadata, truncated contexts, or injected system instructions). This PR introduces a CI/managed-gate that synthesizes cohorts at evaluation time based on semantic context clusters and adaptively reweights alert sign...
text:
Motivation: We frequently see spurious regression signals when prompt distributions shift subtly (new metadata, truncated contexts, or injected system instructions). This PR introduces a CI/managed-gate that synthesizes cohorts at evaluation time based on semantic context clusters and adaptively reweights alert signals so short-term distribution shifts do not produce noisy failures.

What this PR does: 1) Adds a new gate type 'contextual_cohort_synthesis' that can be plugged into the CI eval pipeline; 2) Implements a lightweight vector-backed cohort builder that groups examples by a configurable semantic window (prompt+metadata embedding); 3) Computes weighted regression scores across synthesized cohorts with an attenuation factor tied to cohort volatility; 4) Exposes config knobs (min_cohort_size, volatility_smoothing, fallback_anchor) and a canary rollout policy; 5) Emits detailed t...

title: Enrich on-call diagnostics with automated runbook suggestions and triage helpers
preview: Context: On-call teams often waste critical minutes assembling the same diagnostic surface (pod logs, recent deploys, graph snapshots, SLO deltas) before they can follow runbooks. This PR adds an automated diagnostics gatherer plus a lightweight runbook-suggestion engine to incident-bot so on-call responders get an ...
text: Context: On-call teams often waste critical minutes assembling the same diagnostic surface (pod logs, recent deploys, graph snapshots, SLO deltas) before they can follow runbooks. This PR adds an automated diagnostics gatherer plus a lightweight runbook-suggestion engine to incident-bot so on-call responders get an actionable triage checklist and templated next steps in Slack/PagerDuty threads. Motivation: reduce mean time to acknowledge (MTTA) and mean time to resolution (MTTR) by surfacing high-value signals automatically and recommending runbook steps tailored to the observed signals. Summary of changes: 1) diagnostics worker: new background worker that gathers a bounded set of artifacts (pod logs tails, pod event summaries, recent deploy metadata, SLO deltas from slo-toolkit, topology hints) with configurable TTL and size limits; 2) suggestion engine: rule-based scorer + template ...

title: Introduce Signal Preservation Matrix and latency-attribution grok
preview: Context: long-running and async inference pipelines were losing small-but-critical invocation context across stage boundaries (streaming proxies, sidecars, queued workers). This caused noisy alerts, broken token-level latency attribution, and poor rollback signals during incidents. This PR introduces the Signal Pres...
text: Context: long-running and async inference pipelines were losing small-but-critical invocation context across stage boundaries (streaming proxies, sidecars, queued workers). This caused noisy alerts, broken token-level latency attribution, and poor rollback signals during incidents. This PR introduces the Signal Preservation Matrix (SPM): a compact, TTL-bound context envelope propagated with spans/metrics/logs; and a latency-attribution grok that maps low-level span phases to token-phase buckets so we can attribute cost/latency consistently across stages. Changes include telemetry enrichment in the serving runtime, an ingestion-time marker that tags preserved signals, a small schema migration for the telemetry index to support SPM keys, three dashboard panels for SPM preservation rate / cross-stage latency-bridge / token-phase waterfall, and an interactive alert-sensitivity tuner that ...

title: Ensure atomic SSE delta emission and robust JSON boundary guards for tool/function call streams
preview: Motivation: We observed a class of failures in long-running streaming responses where partial JSON objects emitted as SSE 'delta' fragments could be delivered out-of-order or combined with delimiter anomalies after network retries or proxy replays. This manifested as broken tool/function-call payloads, intermittent ...
text:
Motivation: We observed a class of failures in long-running streaming responses where partial JSON objects emitted as SSE 'delta' fragments could be delivered out-of-order or combined with delimiter anomalies after network retries or proxy replays. This manifested as broken tool/function-call payloads, intermittent JSON parse errors in downstream OpenAI-compat clients, and rare duplicate function-sentinel emissions under high-concurrency bursts. The goal of this PR is to make SSE delta emission atomic with explicit boundary guards so clients can deterministically reassemble structured outputs and tool call payloads even under retries or fragment reordering.

Summary of changes: implemented an atomic delta buffer on the SSE emitter that guarantees each logical token/event batch is emitted as an indivisible unit (either fully emitted or retried in full), introduced locked boundary marke...

title: Introduce fast-feedback lanes for trivial changes and a dependency safety gate
preview: Motivation: CI cycle time has been steadily increasing as we add more integration and security checks. For small, low-risk changes (docs, single-file lint fixes, infra config tweaks) engineers should get feedback in under 5 minutes. At the same time we need an automated, conservative gate for dependency updates to c...
text:
Motivation: CI cycle time has been steadily increasing as we add more integration and security checks. For small, low-risk changes (docs, single-file lint fixes, infra config tweaks) engineers should get feedback in under 5 minutes. At the same time we need an automated, conservative gate for dependency updates to catch transitive supply-chain regressions.

Summary: this PR introduces a small change detection tool and a staged CI workflow that routes PRs to a fast-feedback lane or a full-check lane based on change classification. It also adds a composite dependency-safety action that performs transitive dependency quarantine checks and configurable quarantine policies.

What changed: 1) .github/workflows/staged-ci.yml implements two lanes: fast-path (lint/units/docs + cached build) and full-path (integration, e2e, heavy flakiness-prone tests). 2) tools/change_detector: tiny Rust binar...

title: fp8-autotune-dequant-rewrite-and-kernel-router
preview: Motivation: We observed cache-sensitive and sequence-length-dependent regressions when serving FP8/INT8 model variants on the high-throughput path. Two root causes surfaced in profiling: (1) redundant dequantization stubs inserted by the compiler leading to extra memory traffic across the KV cache boundary, and (2) ...
text:
Motivation: We observed cache-sensitive and sequence-length-dependent regressions when serving FP8/INT8 model variants on the high-throughput path. Two root causes surfaced in profiling: (1) redundant dequantization stubs inserted by the compiler leading to extra memory traffic across the KV cache boundary, and (2) suboptimal kernel selection for mixed FP8/INT8 attention matmuls at mid-sequence lengths, which increases both latency tail and per-token GPU utilization. This PR introduces an autotuned FP8 dequant rewrite pass (dequant-rewriter) plus a scheduler-aware kernel router that picks kernels based on runtime signals (sequence position, KV cache hit pattern, and batching profile).

Summary of changes:
- Implement dequant-rewriter: a graph-level pass that coalesces adjacent dequant ops, hoists dequantization out of hot KV cache paths when safe, and emits a fused "dequant+transpose"...

title: Add GPU thermal-throttle emulator and warmup stability harness
preview: Motivation: Intermittent production incidents traced to GPU thermal/power throttling during long inference bursts caused rare regressions in tail latency and kernel scheduling. We need a reproducible, low-overhead emulator to inject thermal/power profiles into benchmark runs and a repeatable warmup-stability harness...
text:
Motivation: Intermittent production incidents traced to GPU thermal/power throttling during long inference bursts caused rare regressions in tail latency and kernel scheduling. We need a reproducible, low-overhead emulator to inject thermal/power profiles into benchmark runs and a repeatable warmup-stability harness to measure time-to-steady-state under those conditions. This PR introduces a GPU thermal-throttle emulator and a warmup stability harness that: 1) simulates frequency/power capping curves, 2) injects transient throttles at configurable epochs, 3) coordinates with the existing warmup priming runner to capture time-to-convergence and tail variance, and 4) writes compact stability artifacts for downstream analysis.

What changed (high level): - New thermal_emulator/ module (Python) that runs an in-process state machine describing temperature/power curves and emits a small shi...

title: Introduce RAG rehearsal sandbox with step-mocks and eval hooks
preview: Motivation: Add a small, local-first RAG "rehearsal" quickstart that helps engineers and evaluators iterate on end-to-end scenarios deterministically. The sandbox provides: 1) a scenario YAML format for stepwise interactions (queries, tool calls, agent decisions), 2) step-mock harnesses to replace external tools (se...
text:
Motivation: Add a small, local-first RAG "rehearsal" quickstart that helps engineers and evaluators iterate on end-to-end scenarios deterministically. The sandbox provides: 1) a scenario YAML format for stepwise interactions (queries, tool calls, agent decisions), 2) step-mock harnesses to replace external tools (search, embeddings, vector stores) with canned responses, 3) a lightweight CLI runner to replay scenarios locally and capture structured traces, and 4) eval hooks that emit run-level metrics and Golden-expected assertions for CI integration.

This PR introduces the example project, docs, and a test harness.

Checklist:
- [x] Add quickstart scaffolding and README
- [x] Implement scenario schema and runner CLI
- [x] Provide three sample scenarios (happy path, ambiguous retrieval, safety-fallback)
- [x] Add step-mock implementations for search/knn/embeddings
- [x] Integrate tele...

title: Proactive mitigations simulation harness and playbook safety gates
preview: Motivation: On several recent incidents automated mitigations triggered by the incident-bot caused partial service disruption because there was no pre-run simulation of stateful actions and limited safety gating. This PR introduces a lightweight simulation harness that executes mitigation steps against a sandboxed s...
text:
Motivation: On several recent incidents automated mitigations triggered by the incident-bot caused partial service disruption because there was no pre-run simulation of stateful actions and limited safety gating. This PR introduces a lightweight simulation harness that executes mitigation steps against a sandboxed state model and a set of declarative safety gates that must pass before the bot can apply real-world actions.

What this PR does (single-string description): Adds a mitigation simulation harness, a safety-gate DSL for playbooks, a test suite with representative incident scenarios, and small plumbing to record simulation traces in S3 for post-run analysis. Includes documentation, a canary rollout path, CLI helpers (incubate mode) and a feature flag to opt-in per-team.

Checklist: 
- [x] Simulation harness core runtime (deterministic, time-boxed) 
- [x] Safety-gate DSL and eva...

title: introduce-cost-scribe-routing-and-latency-aware-quant-suggester
preview: Problem: Customers with mixed latency/cost SLOs often leave batching/caching/quantization defaults untouched. That produces suboptimal unit economics for tail workloads and bursty patterns. Motivation: introduce a lightweight routing and suggestion layer (Cost Scribe) that records per-route micro-econ signal, uses a...
text: Problem: Customers with mixed latency/cost SLOs often leave batching/caching/quantization defaults untouched. That produces suboptimal unit economics for tail workloads and bursty patterns. Motivation: introduce a lightweight routing and suggestion layer (Cost Scribe) that records per-route micro-econ signal, uses a latency-aware quantization suggester to create conservative quant profiles, and surfaces safe defaults in the Console so product engineers can opt-in without manual experimentation. This PR wires three pieces: 1) A routing heuristic that tags requests with a cost_economy score (serving/router/cost_scribe_router.go). The score is computed from token length histogram, recent latency percentiles, and prefill hit rate. 2) A latency-aware quantization suggester prototype (serving/quant/latency_aware_suggester.py) that produces quant profiles (bits/activation scale) with a safet...

title: presubmit-stabilizer-adds-lightweight-toolchain-images-and-targeted-lint-tenancy
preview: Context and motivation: Presubmit pipelines have grown noisy and slow as more teams enabled heavy linters and end-to-end integration checks. This PR introduces a 'Presubmit Stabilizer'—a set of changes that reduce I/O, shrink toolchain images for fast lanes, and add a tenancy model for linters so teams can opt into ...
text:
Context and motivation: Presubmit pipelines have grown noisy and slow as more teams enabled heavy linters and end-to-end integration checks. This PR introduces a 'Presubmit Stabilizer'—a set of changes that reduce I/O, shrink toolchain images for fast lanes, and add a tenancy model for linters so teams can opt into expensive checks.

Goals:
- Reduce presubmit wall time for common PRs
- Maintain strict checks for teams that need them via opt-in profiles
- Avoid surprising behavior for contributors (opt-in by default for strict checks)

Summary of changes:
1) ci/toolchain/Dockerfile.slim: new slim toolchain image (Debian-slim base, preinstalled minimal build-toolchain, pip/poetry cache layers).
2) .github/workflows/presubmit.yml: split presubmit lanes into fastpath (uses slim image, minimal devdeps) and strictpath (full image, opt-in via LINT_PROFILE label or PRESUBMIT_LINT_PROFILE env)...

title: introduce-ecohistorian-batch-indexer-and-defaults-generator
preview: Motivation: We repeatedly see suboptimal tenant defaults for batching and cache sizing because current heuristics don't leverage historical batch shapes and cross-tenant cache hit signals. This PR adds an offline "ecohistorian" indexer service that ingests anonymized serving telemetry and produces tenant-specific ca...
text:
Motivation: We repeatedly see suboptimal tenant defaults for batching and cache sizing because current heuristics don't leverage historical batch shapes and cross-tenant cache hit signals. This PR adds an offline "ecohistorian" indexer service that ingests anonymized serving telemetry and produces tenant-specific candidate defaults (batch shape templates, KV cache size suggestions, and conservative quantization hints). The goal is automation: feed the optimizer with statistically grounded defaults instead of hand-tuned heuristics.

What this PR does (summary): 1) New service pkg/ecohistorian: a lightweight indexer that summarizes sequence-length distributions, inter-request spacing, prefix reuse, and cache hit rates into compact signatures. 2) Generator module in tools/econ-defaults: consumes historian signatures to output tenant defaults as YAML/JSON (batching.default, cache.size_hin...

title: introduce-budget-mapper-mini-batcher-defaults-and-lightweight-quant-profiles
preview: Motivation: many tenants operate with low to moderate QPS where full tenant-wide prefill and large batch policies add cost without improving tail latency. This PR adds a lightweight 'budget mapper' that emits per-tenant mini-batcher defaults and an opinionated set of small quantization profiles so teams can get cost...
text:
Motivation: many tenants operate with low to moderate QPS where full tenant-wide prefill and large batch policies add cost without improving tail latency. This PR adds a lightweight 'budget mapper' that emits per-tenant mini-batcher defaults and an opinionated set of small quantization profiles so teams can get cost-down defaults that are safe for production.

Summary of changes: adds a BudgetMapper component that infers conservative mini-batching and cache window defaults from historical unit-econ signals (p95 latency, tokens-per-request, cold-start rate). Introduces a tiny, validated quant_profiles.yaml with three compact profiles (tiny-int8, lean-int4, compat-fp16) and a CLI subcommand 'redwood econ inspect' to preview tenant defaults. Defaults are intentionally conservative and include automatic safe fallbacks (no quantization for models flagged as unsafe). The PR wires telemetry ...

title: quickcheck: add lightweight oncall quick-check CLI and compact diagnostic packer
preview: Implements a small, dependency-light quick-check CLI and a compact diagnostic packer that incident responders can run from the incident channel or a runbook step. Motivation: many oncall incidents are prolonged by manual ad-hoc data collection and noisy attachments. This PR provides a safe, opinionated tool that cap...
text: Implements a small, dependency-light quick-check CLI and a compact diagnostic packer that incident responders can run from the incident channel or a runbook step. Motivation: many oncall incidents are prolonged by manual ad-hoc data collection and noisy attachments. This PR provides a safe, opinionated tool that captures a bounded set of artifacts (sanitized logs, pod status, recent traces, KV cache hints) and emits a single compressed artifact suitable for attaching to an incident thread or uploading to the internal diagnostics bucket. Key properties: fast (<= 15s typical), bounded output size (configurable default 8MB), privacy-first sanitization hooks, dry-run mode, and automatic S3-compatible upload with short-lived presigned URLs. Includes a new slash-command integration for incident-bot (/quickcheck) to trigger the CLI remotely via action runners and a small Webhook consumer use...

title: Sync SDKs: auth/retry presets, stream ergonomics, and minor version bumps
preview: Motivation: Multiple product teams reported subtle differences in default auth token refresh timing and retry jitter across SDKs which led to uneven retry storms during rollout tests. This PR unifies a compact credential adapter, aligns retry presets to latency-aware defaults, and polishes streaming ergonomics (paus...
text: Motivation: Multiple product teams reported subtle differences in default auth token refresh timing and retry jitter across SDKs which led to uneven retry storms during rollout tests. This PR unifies a compact credential adapter, aligns retry presets to latency-aware defaults, and polishes streaming ergonomics (pausable iterators / explicit resume API) across Python/TypeScript/Go language bindings. Summary of changes: - Introduce 'cred-compact' adapter used by all SDKs: smaller surface for users who only need default token refresh+prefetch. - Standardize retry presets: default backoff = exponential base 200ms, max backoff 5s, add latency-aware variant that reduces base to 50ms for sub-50ms SLO routes. - Streaming ergonomics: add Iterator.pause()/resume() semantics (py: AsyncIterator.pause_resume; ts: AsyncGeneratorController; go: StreamHandle.Pause/Resume). - Improve typed errors on s...

title: Attenuated warm-pool smoothing and utilization exposer for dedicated fleets
preview: Context: Dedicated fleet warm pools are currently susceptible to short-lived microbursts: a handful of tenants generate transient spikes that cause the warm-pool controller to over-allocate headroom, inflate cost, and produce noisy per-tenant utilization metrics. This PR introduces an attenuated smoothing policy for...
text: Context: Dedicated fleet warm pools are currently susceptible to short-lived microbursts: a handful of tenants generate transient spikes that cause the warm-pool controller to over-allocate headroom, inflate cost, and produce noisy per-tenant utilization metrics. This PR introduces an attenuated smoothing policy for warm-pool sizing and a lightweight utilization exposer that decouples instantaneous spikes from the long-running reservation logic. Motivation: reduce oscillation-driven overprovisioning, improve stability for tenant SLAs, and provide clearer telemetry to platform teams so they can tune reservations and burst contracts. Changes in this PR (high level): - Introduce an attenuated smoothing window with configurable decay factors and min/max headroom bounds. The algorithm applies exponential decay to short spikes while allowing sustained demand to grow warm pool capacity. - Ad...
